client_input.title: Land Acquisition Dispute
client_input.description: Land acquisition dispute in Maharashtra regarding agricultural land

citations_found.case_title: Ramchandra Bhambaji Pawar vs State Of Maharashtra And Ors on 2 March, 2017
citations_found.summary: Legal Document View ] \ , Cited by \] Bombay High Court Ramchandra Bhambaji Pawar vs State Of Maharashtra And Ors on 2 March, 2017 Author: Bench: , ``` skc 1 JUDGMENT-WP-6620-12-GROUP MATTER IN THE HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE AT BOMBAY CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION WRIT PETITION NO. 6620 OF 2012 Genba Laxman Pawagi .. Petitioner versus State of Maharashtra & Ors. .. Respondents WITH WRIT PETITION NO. 6621 OF 2012 Ramu Bhiwa Pawagi by LRs. Ganpat Ramu Pawagi .. Petitioner versus State of Maharashtra ...
citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute was about the implementation of the Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980, and the Maharashtra Government's decision to divert forest land for non-forest purposes without obtaining prior approval from the Central Government. The court decided that the State Government's decision to divert forest land was unlawful and that the affected petitioners were entitled to compensation for the loss of their land.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute was about the acquisition of 3,839 acres of land in Bangalore for the formation of the "Arkavathi Layout" by the Bangalore Development Authority (BDA), which was challenged by landowners on various grounds. The Karnataka High Court ultimately quashed the acquisition proceedings, declaring that the BDA had no jurisdiction to frame developmental schemes in the Bangalore Metropolitan Area. The court's decision was based on the grounds upheld by the learned Single Judge in the impugned judgment under appeal.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute was about the applicability of the Maharashtra Value Added Tax (MVAT) Act, 2002, and the Maharashtra Goods and Services Tax (MGST) Act, 2017, to certain transactions involving financial institutions and asset reconstruction companies. The court ultimately decided that the transactions in question were exempt from both MVAT and MGST, as they were related to the transfer of debts and not the sale of goods or services.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in this case was about whether a particular property was recognized as private property of the Ruler, Devi Ahilyabai Holkar, or not. The Madhya Pradesh High Court decided that the dispute was not adjudicable under Article 363 of the Constitution of India, as it was a matter arising out of the terms of the Covenant.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in this case was about the validity of a government notification issued by the State of Maharashtra, which affected the rights of several petitioners who were members of the Pawagi community. The court ultimately decided that the notification was unconstitutional and directed the state government to take steps to rectify the situation and restore the rights of the affected individuals.
